ground_truth.title: 10 Free Accounting Tools for Small Businesses
ground_truth.author: Jessica Elliott
ground_truth.main_content:
As a small business owner, you know how quickly expenses can add up. Fortunately, you don't have to sacrifice quality to find affordable accounting software. No-fee bookkeeping apps offer basic accounts receivable and invoicing, expense tracking, and bank reconciliation features. This guide explores the 10 best free accounting tools for small businesses and three vendors offering 30-day free trials.

The Wave Starter plan is free forever. It provides intuitive mobile apps and allows unlimited invoices, customers, estimates, products, and vendors. One user can access accounting and invoicing tools through desktop and mobile devices, but it costs extra to capture receipts or connect bank accounts digitally.

It offers a free version for micro-businesses earning $50,000 or less in annual revenue. One user and an accountant can send up to 1,000 invoices and enter 1,000 or fewer expenses yearly. Zoho's free bookkeeping app supports W9 and 1099 reporting and syncs with multiple banks and credit cards.

ZipBooks Starter is free forever for one user. It connects to one bank account, allows unlimited invoicing, and supports Square and PayPal payments. ZipBooks automatically classifies new contacts as vendors or customers and supports unlimited records.

It is free desktop accounting software for businesses with five employees or fewer. Time-saving features like order and invoice automation help small teams with repeat customers. It works on Windows (7 to 11) and macOS 10.5 and above.

It provides free online accounting software for small businesses. The On-Premise Standard Plan allows unlimited invoicing and bill paying. Because Akaunting is open-source, you can modify the code to fit your business needs.

It is a free online accounting system for small businesses with one to five employees. Unlike other free bookkeeping software, Brightbook doesn't track mileage.

It is a free forever accounting platform for Linux, macOS X, and Windows. The database format is standardized, meaning you can transfer files between computers with different operating systems.

It offers free and paid business apps for finance, sales, and human resources. The stand-alone Odoo Accounting app supports unlimited users and synced bank or cash accounts.

You can deploy it on-premise or online.

It is a free, small business accounting tool that runs exclusively on desktops, with Windows, macOS X, and Linux support. It doesn't require a cloud account; no data leaves your device.

If free software for small business accounting doesn't meet your needs, consider a free trial. Leading platforms give you 30 days to explore advanced features.
with: As a small business owner, you know how quickly expenses can add up.
Zoho Books offers a free version for micro-businesses earning $50,000 or less in annual revenue.
If free software for small business accounting doesn't meet your needs, consider a free trial.
GnuCash is a free, small business accounting tool that runs exclusively on desktops.
